title: 10 best herbal capsules for amenorrhea
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for amenorrhea are natural supplements made from plant extracts that help to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms associated with amenorrhea, a condition characterized by the absence or infrequent periods. These capsules work by promoting hormone balance, improving liver function, and nourishing the endocrine system. Examples of herbal capsules that help with amenorrhea include Vitex agnus-castus, Chasteberry, and Dong Quai, which have been shown to increase progesterone levels, regulate estrogen, and stimulate uterine contractions. By using these supplements, women can experience improved menstrual regularity, reduced symptoms of premenstrual syndrome (PMS), and enhanced overall health, leading to a significant improvement in their quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai capsules helps with amenorrhea because they contain a blend of natural herbs that stimulate uterine blood flow, regulate hormonal balance, and alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance. The capsules' active ingredients, such as Angelica sinensis and other botanicals, help to restore regular menstrual cycles by promoting ovulation, thickening the uterine lining, and reducing stress and anxiety. By addressing underlying hormonal imbalances, dong quai capsules can effectively treat amenorrhea and promote a healthy reproductive system.
remedy_properties: Aromatase inhibition: Dong Quai capsules contain compounds that inhibit aromatase, an enzyme responsible for converting androgens to estrogens, which can help regulate hormonal balance and stimulate menstruation.

Vasodilation and blood circulation promotion: The capsule's active compounds, such as ferulic acid and kaempferol, dilate blood vessels and promote blood flow, which helps to improve uterine circulation, reduce inflammation, and prepare the body for a regular menstrual cycle.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Dong Quai capsules contain powerful antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to amenorrhea by disrupting hormonal balance and causing endometrial damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of dong quai are rich in saponins, which help to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of dong quai contain bioactive compounds that stimulate uterine contractions, helping to restore normal menstruation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica sinensis roots in autumn or spring ensuring they are free from contaminants.
Clean the roots by gently scrubbing them with a soft brush and dehydrate at 60°c for 24 hours.
Grind 100g of dried angelica sinensis roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 3g of powdered angelica sinensis with 10ml of vegetable oil and 30ml of ethanol in a heat-resistant container.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared herbal mixture and store them in an airtight container for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damiana capsules helps with amenorrhea because they stimulate uterine contractions, promoting hormonal balance and regular menstrual cycles. The herb's phytoestrogenic properties also help to regulate estrogen levels, which can become imbalanced in cases of amenorrhea. Additionally, damiana's anti-inflammatory effects may reduce pelvic congestion and spasms that can contribute to the condition. By addressing these underlying issues, damiana capsules can help restore a normal menstrual cycle and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Damiana's antioxidant properties may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to hormonal imbalances and amenorrhea by protecting the body from free radical damage.

Hormone-balancing effects: The phytoestrogens and flavonoids present in Damiana capsules have been shown to promote estrogen balance and stimulate ovulation, making it a potential natural remedy for regulating menstrual cycles and treating Amenorrhea.

Uterine stimulant properties: Damiana's ability to stimulate uterine contractions may help regulate menstruation by promoting regular shedding of the uterine lining and relieving symptoms of Amenorrhea such as heavy bleeding or irregular periods.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in regulating hormonal imbalances and menstrual cycles.
Seeds: Rich in fatty acids and phytoestrogens, which help to balance estrogen levels and promote regular menstruation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried turnera diffusa roots and leaves in quantities of 100g for each dose.
Clean the plant material by washing it gently with distilled water and air drying for 30 minutes.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder for 15 minutes.
Mix the powdered herb with 500mg of gelatin capsules and fill to desired dosage of 300mg per capsule.
Seal the filled capsules in airtight containers and store them in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chrysanth capsules helps with amenorrhea because they contain herbs that stimulate hormonal balance, particularly estrogen and progesterone. The capsules' active ingredients, such as Angelica sinensis, have been traditionally used to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of dysmenorrhea. By promoting a healthy balance of hormones, chrysanth capsules can help restore regular ovulation and menstruation in women experiencing amenorrhea. This natural approach can provide relief from the emotional distress associated with irregular periods.
remedy_properties: Emollient and anti-inflammatory properties: These properties can help to soothe and calm the uterus and pelvic area, which may help to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea such as cramps and pain.

Antioxidant and anti-cancer properties: Chrysanthemum capsules contain antioxidants that can help to protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to hormonal imbalances and menstrual irregularities associated with amenorrhea.

Regulatory effects on estrogen and progesterone hormones: Chrysanthemum is believed to have a regulatory effect on estrogen and progesterone hormone production, which can help to restore normal menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea such as irregular periods or delayed menstruation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chrysanthemum roots are used to regulate menstruation and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce uterine contractions.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Chrysanthemum contain sesquiterpenes that have been shown to stimulate blood flow and promote menstruation, making them effective for treating amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh chrysanthemum morifolium flowers and dry them in a cool, dark place for at least two weeks to preserve their potency.
Grind 30 grams of dried chrysanthemum morifolium flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered herb with 10 grams of silica gel in a small bowl to enhance absorption and reduce particle size.
Fill empty capsules with 500 milligrams of the herb-silica mixture, making sure not to overfill them to ensure easy swallowing.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months, or refrigerate them for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with amenorrhea because they contain high concentrations of calcium and magnesium, essential minerals that support hormonal balance in the body. These nutrients help to regulate the pituitary gland, which is responsible for stimulating ovulation and menstrual cycles. The antioxidants present in maidenhair tree extract also help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy uterine function, further contributing to the restoration of normal menstrual cycles. As a result, regular consumption of maidenhair tree capsules can help to alleviate amenorrhea symptoms and restore natural menstruation patterns.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to amenorrhea by regulating hormonal balance and promoting healthy ovulation.

Vasodilatory effects: The flavonoids and terpenes in Ginkgo biloba capsules help dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the uterus and ovaries, which may help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of Amenorrhea such as irregular or absent periods.

Hormone regulation: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to have a regulatory effect on hormone levels, including estrogen and progesterone, which can help restore normal menstrual function and alleviate symptoms of Amenorrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help regulate hormonal balance and alleviate menstrual irregularities associated with amenorrhea.
Barks: Containing tannins and phenolic compounds, which aid in reducing inflammation and promoting uterine health, making them effective in treating amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees.
Dry the harvested leaves using shade drying or air circulation methods for 7-10 days.
Grind dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500mg of the ground ginkgo biloba powder with 100mg of gelatin capsules and fill into size #0 capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony capsules helps with amenorrhea because they contain natural estrogen-regulating compounds that help to stimulate menstruation. The capsules are made from the dried roots of the Chinese Peony plant, which has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to treat gynecological disorders. By promoting hormonal balance and improving blood flow to the uterus, Chinese peony capsules can help to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea. Additionally, the capsules may also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common underlying causes of amenorrhea.
remedy_properties: Warming and nourishing: Herbal Chinese Peony Capsules can warm and nourish the uterus, helping to stimulate blood flow and hormone production, which can help regulate menstrual cycles.

Regulating estrogen levels: The capsules contain flavonoids and other compounds that have been shown to regulate estrogen levels in the body, which is essential for maintaining regular menstrual cycles and alleviating symptoms of amenorrhea such as absent periods or irregular bleeding.

Strengthening uterine contraction: Herbal Chinese Peony Capsules can strengthen contractions of the uterus, helping to stimulate menstrual cramps and flow, which can help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Chinese Peony plant contain a high concentration of starch and other nutrients, making them effective in nourishing the body and regulating menstrual cycles.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the Chinese Peony plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality paeonia lactiflora roots in autumn or early spring after drying and cleaning.
Grind 20g of dried paeonia lactiflora roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered root with 50g of gelatin powder and 200ml of hot water to create a uniform paste, stirring for 5 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the prepared mixture, ensuring each capsule contains approximately 400mg, and seal securely after 5 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature, away from direct sunlight, for up to 2 years before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree capsules helps with amenorrhea because it is rich in phytoestrogens, which can help regulate hormonal imbalances that may be causing menstrual irregularities. The capsules contain a compound called casticin, which has been shown to stimulate the production of luteinizing hormone (LH) and follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH), promoting ovulation and menstruation. Additionally, chaste tree capsules have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce pelvic pain and cramping associated with amenorrhea. By balancing hormones and reducing inflammation, chaste tree capsules can help restore a normal menstrual cycle.
remedy_properties: Agonist effect on progesterone receptors: Chaste tree extract has been shown to stimulate the production of progesterone, which can help regulate menstruation and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.

Hormonal balancing properties: The herb is known to have a regulating effect on hormones, particularly estrogen and progesterone, which can help restore normal menstrual cycles and alleviate amenorrheic symptoms such as irregular or absent periods.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects: Chaste tree extract has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation in the uterus and pelvis, which can contribute to menstrual irregularities and amenorrhea.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of the chaste tree contain flavonoids and terpenes that help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.
Seeds: The seeds of the chaste tree are rich in fatty acids and saponins, which support hormone balance and fertility, making them an effective treatment for amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried vitex agnus castus fruit, ensuring minimal contamination and proper storage conditions.
Grind the dried fruit into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 minutes, producing approximately 100 grams of powder.
Mix the vitex agnus castus powder with 200 grams of gelatin capsules according to the manufacturer's instructions, yielding 400 capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature or refrigerate them for up to two years, ensuring optimal freshness and potency.
Dispense the herbal capsules to patients in dosages of 20-40 mg three times daily, with food and water, for a minimum of six months to optimize amenorrhea relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek capsules helps with amenorrhea because of its unique ability to stimulate the hormonal balance in the body, particularly estrogen levels. The herb contains a compound called saponin, which has been shown to increase estrogen production and improve menstrual regularity. By regulating hormone levels, fenugreek capsules can help induce menstruation and alleviate symptoms such as irregular periods, heavy bleeding, and pelvic pain associated with amenorrhea. Additionally, fenugreek's anti-inflammatory properties may also help soothe the uterine lining, promoting a healthy menstrual cycle.
remedy_properties: Estrogenic activity: Fenugreek contains phytoestrogens, which can mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, helping to regulate hormonal imbalances and promote menstrual regularity.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Fenugreek's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the uterus and cervix, promoting a healthy uterine environment that supports menstruation.

Stimulatory effect on uterine contractions: The saponins present in fenugreek may help stimulate uterine contractions, which can aid in establishing menstrual regularity and treating amenorrhea by triggering or increasing blood flow to the uterus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in estrogenic compounds, helping to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.
Seeds: Containing mucilage, which soothes and nourishes the reproductive system, supporting hormonal balance and menstruation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried trigonella foenum graecum flowers and store them in an airtight container for further processing.
Grind the stored trigonella foenum graecum flowers into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle for about 10 minutes.
Mix 250 grams of the ground trigonella foenum graecum powder with 125 grams of gelatin and 25 grams of glycerin in a bowl, allowing it to blend for 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with approximately 500 milligrams of the trigonella foenum graecum mixture each, and seal them using a capsule filler machine within 2 hours of preparation.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or in the refrigerator for up to 1 year to maintain potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with amenorrhea because they contain curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that has been shown to regulate hormonal imbalances and improve uterine health. The anti-inflammatory properties of curcumin help to reduce inflammation in the uterus, which can lead to irregular periods and amenorrhea. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to protect the endometrium from damage, allowing it to heal and function properly, ultimately leading to regular menstrual cycles.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's main active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the uterus and pelvic area, which may contribute to amenorrhea.

Antioxidant: Curcumin also exhibits strong antioxidant activity, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy menstrual cycle and potentially alleviating symptoms of amenorrhea.

Regulatory: Turmeric has been traditionally used to regulate menstruation and alleviate period-related symptoms such as cramps, bloating, and mood swings. The capsules may help restore normal menstrual function in individuals with amenorrhea.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, which is the underground stem of the turmeric plant, contains high levels of curcuminoids, powerful antioxidants that help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.

Roots: Turmeric roots are rich in bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help normalize hormonal imbalances and promote regular menstruation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric roots curcuma longa in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Wash and peel the roots gently using distilled water to remove dirt and impurities.
Dry the peeled roots in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried turmeric roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of the powdered curcuma longa and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort capsules helps with amenorrhea because they contain hyperforin, a bioactive compound that has been shown to stimulate hormonal balance in the body. This herb has been traditionally used to treat female reproductive issues, including amenorrhea, by increasing levels of estrogen and progesterone, which can help regulate menstrual cycles and restore normal periods. Additionally, St John's wort capsules may also help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common underlying causes of amenorrhea.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: Stimulates blood flow to the uterus, which can help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea such as absent or irregular periods.
Hypotensive effects: Can help lower blood pressure, which is often associated with amenorrhea, and improve overall cardiovascular health, thereby supporting hormonal balance and menstrual regularity.

Antioxidant properties: Has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that may help protect the endometrium from oxidative stress and inflammation, potentially leading to a more regular menstrual cycle.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in hypericin and hyperforin, which help regulate hormonal balance and menstrual cycles.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that aid in uterine health and fertility.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort leaves and flowers and dry them in shade for two weeks to preserve medicinal properties.
Grind dried st john's wort material into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part ground st john's wort with 2 parts cornstarch and store the blend in an airtight container for one week to allow blending.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 200-250mg of the blended mixture and seal immediately to preserve potency.
Store prepared capsules in a cool dark place for up to six months or freeze for longer term storage for use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with amenorrhea because they can help regulate hormonal imbalances, which is often the root cause of this condition. The capsules contain natural compounds that have a calming effect on the uterine lining, reducing inflammation and spasms that can disrupt menstrual cycles. Additionally, peppermint has been shown to increase blood flow to the uterus, promoting healthy ovulation and menstruation. By addressing hormonal imbalances and uterine health, herbal peppermint capsules may help stimulate regular menstrual periods and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.
remedy_properties: Digestive Relief: Herbal peppermint capsules can help alleviate digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), which is often linked to amenorrhea due to its impact on hormone regulation and gut-brain communication. This property helps relieve symptoms that may be contributing to amenorrhea.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce inflammation in the body, which may be contributing to hormonal imbalances and irregular periods associated with amenorrhea. By reducing inflammation, peppermint may help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.

Mentholic Compounds: The mentholic compounds present in peppermint capsules have been shown to have a gentle stimulant effect on the uterus, which can help restore regular contractions and stimulate menstruation in cases of amenorrhea caused by hormonal imbalances or uterine issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which help regulate hormonal balance and menstrual cycles.
Barks: Contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce cramps and discomfort associated with amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh peppermint leaves mentha x piperita from a pesticide-free area at dawn.
Clean and dry the harvested peppermint leaves in a warm oven at 40°c for 30 minutes.
Blend 15g of dried peppermint leaves with 100ml of cold-pressed sunflower oil in a coffee grinder for 1 minute.
Filter the blended mixture through a cheesecloth into a glass jar, squeezing out as much oil as possible within 10 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of the filtered peppermint extract and store them in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with amenorrhea because they contain apigenin, a flavonoid that has been shown to stimulate the uterus and promote hormonal balance. This can help regulate menstrual cycles and reduce symptoms of dysmenorrhea, such as cramping and heavy bleeding. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe the uterine lining, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy environment for ovulation. By addressing these underlying issues, chamomile capsules can help restore normal menstrual function and alleviate the distress associated with amenorrhea.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Chamomile's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the uterus, which may contribute to menstrual irregularities and amenorrhea.

Hormone-regulating properties: Chamomile contains flavonoids, which have been shown to regulate hormonal imbalances, including estrogen and progesterone levels, that may be associated with amenorrhea.
Sedative and calming effects: Chamomile's sedative properties can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common underlying causes of menstrual irregularities and amenorrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin and luteolin, the flavonoids present in chamomile roots help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms associated with amenorrhea.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers contain a high concentration of azulene, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to soothe uterine spasms, making it beneficial for treating amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried flowers of matricaria chamomilla ensuring 100 grams.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Combine the powdered matricaria chamomilla with 200 grams of tapioca starch and mix well for 5 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with one-half teaspoon of the mixture weighing 500 milligrams each taking 15 minutes.
Store the herbal capsules in airtight containers protecting from moisture and light for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with amenorrhea because of its ability to regulate hormonal imbalances in the body. The herb contains compounds that help to ease stress and anxiety, which are common underlying causes of amenorrhea. By promoting relaxation and reducing tension, lemon balm can help to normalize menstrual cycles and stimulate ovulation. Additionally, lemon balm's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to alleviate any pelvic discomfort or cramping associated with amenorrhea, making it a natural and effective supplement for women seeking to restore their periods.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal lemon balm capsules contain antioxidants that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to amenorrhea by improving hormonal balance and regulating menstrual cycles.

Hormone-regulating properties: Lemon balm has been traditionally used to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of PMS, including cramps, bloating, and mood swings, which may help alleviate amenorrhea caused by hormonal imbalances.

Anti-anxiety and stress-relieving properties: Lemon balm's calming effects on the nervous system can help reduce anxiety and stress, which are common underlying causes of amenorrhea, and promote a healthier menstrual cycle.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in essential oils and flavonoids, which help to regulate hormonal balance and ease menstrual cramps associated with amenorrhea.
Leaves: Contain rosmarinic acid, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that may help alleviate symptoms of dysmenorrhea and support uterine health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh melissa officinalis leaves on a sunny morning in mid-summer.
Clean the harvested leaves by gently rinsing them with cold water and pat drying with paper towels.
Dry the cleaned leaves using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 8 hours to preserve their volatile oils.
Combine 150g of dried melissa officinalis leaves with 50g of capsules in a glass grinder and grind into a fine powder.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 250mg of the prepared powder and seal tightly to ensure freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with amenorrhea because they stimulate blood flow, improve circulation, and relax uterine muscles. The bioactive compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce menstrual cramps and spasms associated with primary or secondary amenorrhea. Additionally, ginger has been shown to regulate hormonal balances and promote healthy ovulation, ultimately helping to restore normal menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Herbal ginger capsules can help alleviate cramps and spasms associated with amenorrhea, which can be caused by hormonal imbalances or other factors.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, may help reduce inflammation in the uterus and pelvic area, which can contribute to menstrual irregularities like amenorrhea.

Stomach-soothing and digestive benefits: Ginger has natural soothing effects on the stomach, which can help alleviate nausea and bloating that often accompany menstruation-related issues like amenorrhea.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, of the ginger plant contain compounds that help to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms associated with amenorrhea.

Roots: The roots of ginger have been traditionally used to treat various gynecological disorders, including amenorrhea, due to their ability to stimulate uterine contractions and improve blood circulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried ginger rhizomes of zingiber officinale in sufficient quantities for preparation.
Grind the dried ginger rhizomes into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powdered ginger with 500 milliliters of distilled water to create an infusion.
Steep the mixture for 10 minutes before filtering it through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove solids.
Fill gelatin capsules with the filtered infusion and seal tightly for storage and administration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle capsules helps with amenorrhea because they contain compounds that stimulate uterine contractions, thereby promoting menstrual regularity. The capsules' bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and triterpenes, also help to regulate hormonal imbalances, which are often at the root of amenorrhea. Additionally, nettle's anti-inflammatory properties may alleviate any underlying endometrial inflammation or irritation that could be contributing to irregular periods, allowing for a more normal menstrual cycle to resume.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in stinging nettle capsules can help reduce inflammation in the uterus, cervix, and ovaries, which may be contributing to amenorrhea, promoting a healthy uterine environment for menstruation to occur.

Hormone-regulating effects: Stinging nettle capsules contain phytochemicals that have been shown to mimic the actions of estrogen and progesterone in the body, helping to regulate hormonal imbalances that can lead to amenorrhea, promoting a more regular menstrual cycle.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, stinging nettle roots are valued for their ability to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.
Rhyzomes: With its high concentration of calcium and magnesium, stinging nettle rhyzomes are believed to help stabilize hormone levels and promote regular ovulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried urtica dioica leaves and stems from a trusted source.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar.
Mix 10 grams of the powdered urtica dioica with 100ml of water to form a liquid extract.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes at room temperature then filter out the solids and retain the clear liquid extract.
Fill capsules with 500mg of the herbal extract and store in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass capsules helps with amenorrhea because of its natural ability to stimulate uterine contractions and regulate hormonal balance. The essential oils present in lemongrass have been shown to increase estrogen levels, which can help normalize menstrual cycles. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of lemongrass may reduce uterine inflammation that can contribute to amenorrhea. By promoting a healthy uterine environment and hormone balance, herbal lemongrass capsules may help alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea and restore regular menstruation.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass contains antioxidants like flavonoids and terpenes that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to Amenorrhea.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in lemongrass capsules may help alleviate symptoms of Amenorrhea by reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding.
Uterine stimulatory properties: Lemongrass has been traditionally used to stimulate uterine contractions, which can help regulate menstruation and alleviate symptoms of Amenorrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, lemongrass roots have been traditionally used to treat menstrual disorders and stimulate uterine contractions.
Rhyzomes: With their unique blend of essential oils, rhyzomes help regulate hormonal balance and alleviate symptoms associated with amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried cymbopogon citratus leaves weighing 200 grams for further processing.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix one part of the powdered cymbopogon citratus with two parts of lactose powder in a clean bowl for 30 seconds.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the mixture weighing approximately 250 milligrams per capsule and seal tightly for 1 minute.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or refrigerate for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sesame capsules helps with amenorrhea because they contain sesamin, a natural compound that has been shown to stimulate hormonal balance and regulate menstrual cycles. Herbal sesame capsules have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various women's health issues, including amenorrhea. The capsules' ability to nourish the reproductive system and promote hormone production can help restore regular menstruation and alleviate symptoms associated with amenorrhea, such as irregular periods, heavy bleeding, or no bleeding at all.
remedy_properties: Emmenagogue: Sesame oil has been traditionally used to stimulate menstruation, promote blood flow, and relieve menstrual cramps, making it an effective emmenagogue for treating amenorrhea caused by hormonal imbalances.

Antioxidant and Anti-inflammatory properties: Sesame oil's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the uterus and endometrium, promoting a healthy uterine lining and restoring normal menstrual cycles.

Prostaglandin-enhancing activity: Sesame oil has been shown to enhance prostaglandin production, which helps regulate menstrual cycles, alleviate cramps, and increase blood flow to the uterus, making it an effective treatment for amenorrhea caused by hormonal imbalances or uterine issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in regulating hormonal imbalances associated with amenorrhea.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids and vitamins, they help stimulate blood flow and promote uterine health, addressing underlying causes of amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality sesamum indicum seeds from a trusted supplier or grow your own.
Dry the sesamum indicum seeds in a warm, dry place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried sesamum indicum seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500mg of the sesamum indicum powder with 250ml of distilled water and heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 30 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared herbal extract and store them in an airtight container in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas Coneflower contains compounds that reduce inflammation and alleviate menstrual cramps, which can contribute to amenorrhea caused by endometrial hypoplasia or pelvic inflammatory disease.

Antioxidant properties: The herb's antioxidant properties help protect the body against oxidative stress and damage, which may improve uterine function and regularity of menstruation, thereby addressing amenorrhea caused by hormonal imbalances or poor circulation.

Estrogen-regulating properties: Kansas Coneflower contains isoflavones that have been shown to regulate estrogen levels and balance the body's hormonal system, which can help alleviate symptoms of polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS), a common cause of amenorrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Kansas coneflower contain compounds that help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea, making them an effective component in medicinal capsules.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which have been shown to improve uterine health and support hormone regulation, making them a valuable addition to amenorrhea treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality echinacea angustifolia roots in late summer or early fall when they are mature and dry.
Dry the harvested roots using a dehydrator at 150°f for 24 hours or by spreading them thinly on paper bags for 7-10 days.
Chop the dried echinacea angustifolia roots into small pieces and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the powdered echinacea angustifolia root with 1 tablespoon of organic cornstarch to create a smooth filling for the capsules.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the prepared mixture, and store them in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with amenorrhea because they contain a natural compound called Echinacea, which has been traditionally used to promote hormonal balance in women. The herb is believed to stimulate the pituitary gland and ovaries, regulating menstrual cycles and encouraging normal bleeding patterns. Additionally, Kansas coneflower's anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe uterine cramps and discomfort associated with amenorrhea. By addressing underlying hormonal imbalances and inflammation, herbal Kansas coneflower capsules may help alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea and promote a healthy reproductive system.

Marshmallow capsules helps with amenorrhea because they promote a healthy endometrial lining, which is essential for regular menstruation. The mucilage in marshmallow root acts as a natural emollient, soothing and protecting the uterine lining, thereby encouraging the growth of new tissue. Additionally, marshmallow's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with amenorrhea, creating a conducive environment for menstrual cycles to return to normal. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to support women's reproductive health and promote regular menstruation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow root has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the pelvic area, which may contribute to amenorrhea by relaxing the uterine muscles and improving blood flow.

Softpith-protective properties: Marshmallow root contains mucilages that can soothe and protect the soft tissues of the reproductive tract, helping to reduce irritation and inflammation that may lead to menstrual irregularities or amenorrhea.

Emmenagogic properties: Marshmallow root has been traditionally used as an emmenagogue, which means it helps to stimulate blood flow and uterine contractions, potentially aiding in the return of menstrual cycles.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating the pain and discomfort associated with amenorrhea.
Fruits: The fruits of the marshmallow plant contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps to soothe and calm irritated tissues, providing relief from menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marshmallow root from a reputable supplier or your own garden in the morning after dew has dried.
Clean and dry the harvested roots thoroughly by gently brushing off dirt and debris and placing them on paper towels.
Chop 250 grams of the cleaned and dried marshmallow root into small pieces and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500 milliliters of boiling water with 10 grams of the powdered marshmallow root and steep for 15 minutes to create an infusion, then strain it through cheesecloth or a coffee filter.

Fill empty gelatin capsules with a mixture of 250 milligrams of the cooled infused liquid and 125 milligrams of dried powdered vitamin c and store them in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milkweed capsules helps with amenorrhea because they contain phytoestrogens, which are plant-based compounds that mimic the effects of estrogen in the body. Herbal milkweed capsules have been traditionally used to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of hormone imbalances, including amenorrhea. The capsules work by stimulating the production of follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) and luteinizing hormone (LH), which are essential for ovulation and menstruation. By rebalancing estrogen levels, milkweed capsules can help induce a normal menstrual cycle and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Milkweed contains a variety of saponins, which have been shown to have estrogenic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to regulate hormonal imbalances and alleviate menstrual cramps, often associated with amenorrhea.
Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids in milkweed capsules may help to stimulate uterine contractions, promoting menstrual flow and regular periods, which can be disrupted in cases of amenorrhea.

Flavonoids and phenolic acids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance, such as irregular or absent menstrual cycles, by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of milkweed contain a high concentration of saponins, which have been shown to stimulate uterine contractions and improve menstrual flow, making them an effective treatment for amenorrhea.
Rhyzomes: Milkweed rhyzomes are rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that can help regulate hormonal imbalances and promote menstrual regularity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried asclepias syriaca root from a reputable supplier or grow your own using organic methods.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of the powdered asclepias syriaca with 40 grams of fillers such as silica or calcium carbonate in a bowl.
Fill #00 gelatin capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the mixture and seal tightly using a capsule filler machine.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to one year.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat amenorrhea with herbal capsules is by combining remedies that regulate hormones and stimulate menstruation. Dong quai, vitex, and chasteberry extract work synergistically to balance estrogen and progesterone levels, while black cohosh and evening primrose oil reduce stress and promote ovulation. Take 2-3 capsules twice daily for 3-6 months, monitoring hormone levels and adjusting dosages as needed. Consult a healthcare professional for personalized guidance.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to amenorrhea that are treated with herbal capsules are polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS), endometriosis, and dysmenorrhea. Herbal remedies like vitex, chasteberry, and turmeric may help regulate hormonal imbalances and alleviate symptoms such as irregular periods, pelvic pain, and heavy bleeding. These herbs may also reduce inflammation and improve overall reproductive health, making them a natural and effective alternative to conventional medications.
